subject: Product Management
title: Microsoft Clippy
description: A micro-lesson that makes Microsoft Clippy usable.

hook.text: 'It looks like you're writing a letter. Would you like help?' Clippy became the most hated feature in software history.

buildup.text: Microsoft added the Office Assistant in 1997 to help new users navigate Word's overwhelming feature set.

discovery.text: The idea was sound: intelligent help that appeared when you needed it. The execution was catastrophically annoying.

twist.text: Clippy interrupted constantly, guessed wrong, and was nearly impossible to dismiss permanently. Users despised it.

climax.text: Microsoft killed Clippy in 2003. The lesson: helping users is good, but interrupting them with bad guesses is worse than no help.

punchline.text: Good intent plus bad execution equals a product disaster.

quiz.question: Why did Microsoft Clippy fail?
options: It interrupted constantly with wrong guesses — annoying users instead of helping
Users didn't need any help with Microsoft Office
The animation was too slow to render
